name: react-luau-props-helper
description: Customizable prop autocomplete for React-Luau createElement calls.

reactLuauPropsHelper.props.markdownDescription:
Per-class prop list. Each entry overrides the built-in defaults for that class — presence of a key is authoritative, so an explicit empty array (`[]`) disables completions for that class. Custom component identifiers (e.g. `MyButton`) can be added here too. Example:

```json
{
  "TextLabel": ["Text", "TextColor3"],
  "MyButton": ["label", "onClick", "disabled"]
}
```

reactLuauPropsHelper.createElementAliase.markdownDescription: Function names treated as React-Luau `createElement` calls. Each entry must be a Lua identifier (dots allowed). Add your own aliases here, e.g. `"r"` if you use `local r = React.createElement`.

enumDescriptions: Insert `Name = ` and place the cursor after the equals sign.
Insert `Name = ,` with the first tab stop at the value and the final tab stop after the comma (recommended for Lua table syntax).
reactLuauPropsHelper.snippetMode.markdownDescription: Controls what gets inserted when you accept a completion.

scripts.pretest: npm run compile && npm run lint
